close_reason: Implemented inspectPlayerLocked: bumping another player shows their name, class, HP, and equipped gear. 3 tests added.
design:
## Implementation Plan

### What happens now
When a player bumps into another player, `TryMove` returns `(MoveAttack, otherPlayerID)`. The code at server.go:416 checks `s.isPlayerEntity(target)` and silently returns — no message, no interaction.

### What we want
When a player bumps another player, show the bumper an inspection message with:
- Player name and class
- Current HP / Max HP
- Equipped items (each slot that has something equipped)

The bumped player is unaffected (no message, no state change).

### Changes

**1. `internal/mud/server.go` — `processActionLocked`**
Replace the silent `return` at the `isPlayerEntity(target)` check (~line 416) with a call to a new `inspectPlayerLocked(sess, target)` method.

**2. `internal/mud/server.go` — new `inspectPlayerLocked(sess *Session, targetID ecs.EntityID)`**
- Look up target session via `s.sessionByPlayerID(targetID)`
- If nil, return (shouldn't happen but defensive)
- Get target's Health component from the floor's World
- Build message lines:
  - Line 1: `"👤 <Name> — <ClassName> (<CurrentHP>/<MaxHP> ❤️)"`
  - Line 2: equipment summary — iterate Head/Body/Feet/MainHand/OffHand slots on target's Inventory component; list equipped item names with glyphs, or skip if nothing equipped
- Send via `sess.AddMessage()` (only the bumper sees it)

**3. `internal/mud/mud_test.go` — new test `TestInspectPlayerOnBump`**
- Create server with two sessions on the same floor
- Move player 1 into player 2's position
- Assert player 1 received an inspection message containing player 2's name and class
- Assert player 2 received no messages
- Test with equipment: equip an item on player 2, bump again, verify equipment shows in message
title: On the MUD server when a player moves into another play, the player name, class, health and equipment should be listed. The player who was bumped into should not have anything happen to them.

Let's create a leveling system with unique skill selections for each class. Each level the player can select one of 3 unique skills that are passive or active. When leveling, attack, defence and hp go up aproprpiately for each class

close_reason: Implemented NPC movement system: CNPCMovement component (type 17), ProcessNPCMovement system with wander/path/return/stationary behaviors, time-of-day scheduling (DayCycleTicks=6000), 14 NPC schedules in assets/city.go, wired into city floor and server tick loop. 10 new tests all passing.
description: Add CNPCMovement component (type 17), movement system (wander/path/return/stationary), time-of-day scheduling, and wire it into city NPCs
title: Implement NPC movement system with daily schedules

close_reason: Telnet support fully implemented: handler, TTY adapter, IAC filter, protocol negotiation, tests. Telnet clients share the same MUD world as SSH users on port 2323.
title: The MUD server should also allow telnet connections to the server. They should share the same play area as the SSH users.

We are going to to create a world builder agent. WORLD.md will be the guide that we create for this world. We will read the LORE.md file and use it to create playable maps, randomized dungeons, vibrant cities with themes. The purpose of the WORLD.md file will be to guide the agent when it creates the world.

description: We are going to create a NPC generator agent guided by NPC.md  We should look at the WORLD.md and the LORE.md files and determine what sort of creatures and denizens inhabit the spaces created. some should be friendly, some should be neutral, some should be hostile. The Lore files should tell us about the past present and future of a place that will determine who lives where. The WORLD.md should tell us about how they live. Ecosystems should be conceived
title: create NPC.md for NPC agent

close_reason: LORE.md created — 17,773 words, 13 sections, all canonical facts verified
title: We are going to create a seperate lore agent. It writes the lore of this world. We are going to expand this world out farther. We will create a LORE.md file that guides the lore agent to the past, present and future of the world. It will populate the world with people, regions, relics, relgions, creatures, goods, evils and everything in between. We should draw from science fantasy tropes and create a rich and in-depth lore weaved with many threads of stories.

close_reason: Implemented telnet support: internal/telnet/ package (proto, filter, tty, handler) + 22 tests + --telnet-port flag in cmd/server
description: Add a telnet listener alongside SSH. New internal/telnet/ package with IAC filter, TelnetTty adapter, and HandleConnection. Wire into cmd/server/main.go with --telnet-port flag.
title: Add telnet support to MUD server

close_reason: Already fully implemented: CNPCMovement component, ProcessNPCMovement system, daily schedules for all city NPCs (wander/path/stationary behaviors), integrated into MUD tick loop, tests passing.
title: The townspeople should move around. some should follow paths, others should stay in the same room where appropriate.
